verdict_payload.notes: All five doctrinally-load-bearing terms are preserved: yoginām api sarveṣām (with the Shankara-vs-Ramanuja grammatical split — partitive genitive vs ablative-of-comparison — explicitly noted in disagreements), mad-gatena antar-ātmanā (with inner self gone into Me), śraddhāvān (faithful), bhajate (verb of devoted service, root bhaj), yukta-tama (most yoked). The verse is correctly tagged STRETCHED at the theistic-devotional reach. The engineering analog explicitly names the scope-mismatch upfront and operates only on the structural rung (most-bound to chosen worthy object). Both Shankara's and Ramanuja's readings are cited without conflation. The bhajate verb's full doctrinal weight (root bhaj — to share, serve, worship with devotion) is preserved. PASS.

verdict_payload.real_insight: Two operationally-equivalent senior engineers diverge in stability under career-leveling stress because their interiors are yoked to different objects. The engineer yoked to her own advancement destabilizes; the engineer yoked to what the work serves does not. Implication: when interviewing senior candidates, probe what the interior is yoked to. The candidate who can name the customer, the system, the team in concrete terms is structurally more stable than the candidate whose answers cycle back to title and trajectory.
verdict_payload.objection: I went looking for 'be married to your job.' The verse and the counter-example explicitly disqualify that read: 'workaholic over-identification — the engineer whose interior collapses when her project is killed because her sense-of-self was fused with the project — the verse does not endorse this.' The yoking the verse describes survives the loss of the local instance. wisdom_theater_score=1 because adjacent corporate-loyalty narratives risk co-opting the verse; the engineering layer's explicit guard rails prevent this. PASS.

verdict_payload.actionable_predicate: When evaluating senior IC candidates, add an interior-yoking probe to the interview rubric. Ask: name three customers/users by attribute or story; name three system invariants you'd defend against any roadmap pressure; name three teammates whose growth you've watched. Candidates who can answer concretely are yoked outward. Candidates who answer in abstractions or whose answers cycle back to themselves are yoked inward.
verdict_payload.example_decision: Two staff candidates with equivalent technical signal. Candidate A names three customers, two system invariants, four teammates whose growth she's watched. Candidate B's answers are abstract — 'our customers,' 'our systems,' 'the team.' Hire A. Predicted at next career-leveling event: A holds level, B destabilizes.

verdict_payload.actionable_predicate: Interior-yoking probe in senior-IC interviews. Falsifiable: candidate's answers either name concrete customers/systems/teammates, or do not.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: Critical: the Shankara-vs-Ramanuja grammatical split on yoginām (partitive genitive vs ablative-of-comparison) is explicitly preserved in disagreements. The doctrinal stake is named: for Shankara the bhakta-yogi is the most-yoked among yogis; for Ramanuja the bhakta-yogi is structurally above all other categories of yogi. The engineering layer does not collapse the two readings or take a side; it operates on the structural claim that survives both readings (most-bound to chosen object is most-yoked). bhajate is preserved with full doctrinal weight. śraddhā is preserved as the technical term, not flattened to 'belief.' PASS.
yoginām, mad-gatena, antar-ātmanā, śraddhāvān, bhajate, yukta-tama all preserved with their Sanskrit form alongside engineering gloss. PASS.

verdict_payload.satire_attempt: Local Engineer Renames Personal Brand to 'mad-gatena antar-ātmanā,' Adds 'most yoked, in My opinion' to LinkedIn Headline. New 'Yukta-Tama Leadership Conference' coming to SF in Q3, $4,400 per ticket, sponsored by a customer-success-platform SaaS. CEO promises every attendee will leave 'most-yoked.'
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The satire only lands if you ignore the explicit guard against engineering-devotion equivalence: 'an engineer who reads this verse as a defense of being-married-to-the-job has misread it.' The conference satire requires conflating engineering-yoking with bhakti-yoking, which the falsifiability explicitly disqualifies. The verse is honestly tagged STRETCHED. PASS.

verse_specific_anchors: the explicit hinge-into-bhakti-yoga structure: '6.47 is the chapter's closing verse and the bridge into Chapter 7' is the verse's structural role
the two-engineer contrast around career-leveling stress (yoked-to-self vs yoked-to-served-thing) is shaped to the verse's most-yoked claim
Shankara/Ramanuja's grammatical split on yoginām (partitive vs ablative) is the verse-specific philological flashpoint
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: Tested against 12.6-7 (bhakti-yoga proper, but that comes later), 9.22 (those who worship me with devotion, but that's about the Lord's care, not the most-yoked claim), 18.66 (sarva-dharmān parityajya, the closing instruction — but that's release-of-dharmas, not faith-yoked-most). The bridge-into-Chapter-7 + most-yoked-among-yogis structure is 6.47-specific. PASS.

top_candidates.reasoning: The two-principal-engineer contrast around career-leveling stress, the explicit naming of 'mad-gatena antar-ātmanā' as the verse's structural claim, and the chapter-closing-bridge-into-bhakti framing all point to 6.47. The most-yoked / yukta-tama vocabulary is preserved verbatim.
Possible because 12.6 also valorises bhakti — but the chapter-closing position and yukta-tama-specifically vocabulary are 6.47.

verdict_payload.chapter_voice_summary: BG 6.47 closes the chapter in the senior-teacher's grave-and-warm register. The voice acknowledges the destination beyond engineering reach without flattening it ('the verse's reach exceeds the analog; the chapter does not close where engineering closes'). The two-engineer contrast is delivered specifically and concretely without slipping into hagiography. The implication ('notice what your interior is actually yoked to') lands with weight. PASS.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: MEDIUM + STRETCHED. The theistic-devotional register is explicitly named as exceeding engineering reach. The bhakti is not collapsed into engineering passion; the engineering analog operates on a strictly downscaled rung (most-bound-to-served-thing). Counter-example explicitly refuses workaholic over-identification. Zero trivialization. PASS.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason_external: The verse's full theistic register is preserved as the source's reading; the engineering analog sits beside it at strictly downscaled scope. The chapter-closing claim that 'the chapter does not close where engineering closes' is the inverse of tech-manual framing. PASS.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: Two substantive entries: (1) bhajate — root bhaj, with Prabhupada's 'transcendental loving service,' Shankara's 'adoration with inner self fixed on Vasudeva,' Ramanuja's 'bhakti as highest yoga.' (2) yoginām api sarveṣām — the genitive-vs-ablative grammatical split, which encodes the doctrinal difference (most-yoked-among-yogis for Shankara vs structurally-above-all-yogis for Ramanuja). Both entries identify philological feature, translator pattern, and doctrinal stake. Score 9. PASS.

verdict_payload.reconstructed_sketch: Given chapter context (chapter closing, 6.46 hierarchy claim just delivered), an engineering analog at position 47 would predictably make a closing claim about devotion or faith. A competent writer might write 'devote yourself to the work' — but they would not necessarily land on the specific most-yoked-among-yogis structure or preserve the Shankara/Ramanuja grammatical split.
verdict_payload.verse_specific_signal: The two-engineer contrast around career-leveling stress (yoked-to-self vs yoked-to-served-thing) and the explicit chapter-closing-bridge-into-bhakti framing are 6.47-specific.

verdict_payload.reproducible_artifact: Senior-IC interview rubric extension: interior-yoking probe. Three concrete prompts — name three customers by story, name three system invariants you would defend, name three teammates whose growth you have watched. Scored on concreteness vs abstraction.
verdict_payload.embodiment_sketch: Greenhouse / Lever interview-kit adds the interior-yoking probe section. Hiring manager runs it; rubric scored on a 1-3 scale. Decision-meeting uses it. Concretely copyable Monday.

anchored_threads.thread_name: The struggle and consolation of practice (closing) → bridge into Chapter 12 bhakti
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: 6.47 is the chapter's structural close and the explicit bridge into Chapter 7 / Chapter 12 bhakti elaboration. The chapter-thesis statement names this exactly: 'the closing verse, which valorizes devotion to Krishna... can preserve the structural claim (faith-yoked practice surpasses unfaithful practice) but the verse's full devotional register exceeds operational scope. STRETCHED.' The engineering layer's faith-yoking-to-served-thing is the precise rung the chapter-thesis identified.

progression_issue.explanation: Verse-ordering: 6.47 is the structural close. Correct.
